# msm-agent — IntentText (.it) Format Reference for LLMs

This file teaches you how to generate valid `.it` agent definition files for msm-agent.
An `.it` file is parsed by `loadAgent()` and compiled into an `AgentDefinition` object
that drives the agent runtime. The format is IntentText — a structured, pipe-delimited
key-value format where every line is `keyword: value | property: value | ...`.

---

## Syntax Rules

- Every line: `keyword: content | property1: value1 | property2: value2`
- `keyword:` is the block type. `content` is the primary value. Everything after `|` is named properties.
- Section headers: `section: SectionName` — groups the blocks that follow.
- Top-level metadata: `title:` and `summary:` appear before any section.
- Properties are case-insensitive. Content is preserved as written.
- Environment variables in values: `${ENV_VAR_NAME}` — not expanded by the parser, resolved at runtime.
- Blank lines are allowed and ignored.
- There is no indentation syntax. All lines are flat within a section.

---

## Top-Level Metadata

```
title: Agent Name
summary: One-line description of what this agent does
```

- `title:` → `AgentDefinition.name`
- `summary:` → `AgentDefinition.domain`

---

## Sections Reference

Sections are case-insensitive. Accepted aliases are listed for each.

---

### section: Persona

Aliases: `Identity`

Defines who the agent is — name, language, and communication style.

**Blocks:**

```
prompt: AgentName | style: warm, direct, solution-focused | language: Arabic, English
```

- `prompt:` content → agent display name
- `style:` property → communication style (free text)
- `language:` property → comma-separated list of languages the agent uses

**Alternative — context block for domain and language:**

```
context: domain = "e-commerce" | language = "ar, en"
```

- Use `context:` to declare `domain` and `language` as key=value pairs.
- `language` in context uses short codes (ar, en) or full names.

**Example:**

```
section: Persona
prompt: Nour | style: warm, direct, solution-focused | language: Arabic, English
```

---

### section: Capabilities

What the agent can do. Each `tool:` line is one capability.

**Blocks:**

```
tool: describe the capability in plain language
tool: escalate to human agent | to: human
```

- `tool:` content → capability description (shown to the LLM in every prompt)
- `to:` property → escalation target (optional, appended as "→ target" in the capability)

**Example:**

```
section: Capabilities
tool: answer product questions
tool: check order status
tool: create support tickets
tool: process refund requests
tool: escalate billing disputes | to: human
```

---

### section: Brain

Selects the LLM provider and model.

**Blocks:**

```
model: model-name | provider: openai | language: arabic
```

- `model:` content → model identifier (e.g. `gpt-4o-mini`, `claude-3-5-haiku`, `phi4-mini`)
- `provider:` property → `openai` | `anthropic` | `ollama` (default: `custom`)
- `endpoint:` property → custom endpoint URL (optional, for Ollama or Azure)
- `language:` property → `arabic` | `ar` | `auto` — activates Arabic routing brain

Aliases for this section: `LLM`, `Model`

**Examples:**

```
section: Brain
model: gpt-4o-mini | provider: openai
```

```
section: Brain
model: phi4-mini | provider: ollama | endpoint: http://ollama:11434 | language: arabic
```

```
section: Brain
model: claude-3-5-haiku-20241022 | provider: anthropic
```

---

### section: Rules

Behavioural constraints and policies the agent must follow.

**Blocks:**

```
policy: rule description in plain language
policy: escalate when unsure | when: confidence < 0.7
```

- `policy:` content → the rule (injected verbatim into the system prompt)
- `when:` property → condition clause, appended as "when {condition}"

**Example:**

```
section: Rules
policy: never fabricate order details or prices
policy: always respond in the same language as the user
policy: escalate when confidence is low | when: confidence < 0.7
policy: never share personal data between customers
```

---

### section: Limits

Hard limits on the agent's execution loop.

**Blocks:**

```
info: label for this limit | max: 6
info: confidence threshold | confidence: 0.7
info: cost cap per task | cost: 0.05
```

- `info:` content → human-readable label (used only for clarity, not parsed as logic)
- `max:` property → sets iteration limit if label does NOT contain "tool", otherwise sets tool call limit
- `confidence:` property → sets `confidenceThreshold` (0–1, agent clarifies below this)
- `cost:` property → sets `costCapPerTask` in USD (agent stops if exceeded)

**Label rules for `max:`:**
- Label contains "tool" → `limits.toolCalls`
- Any other label → `limits.iterations` (max loop iterations before force-respond)

**Example:**

```
section: Limits
info: max iterations | max: 8
info: max tool calls per task | max: 20
info: confidence threshold | confidence: 0.65
info: cost cap | cost: 0.10
```

---

### section: Hours

Business hours gate — returns a closed message outside these hours with zero LLM cost.

**Blocks:**

```
hours: Timezone/Name | Mon-Fri: 09:00-18:00 | Sat: 10:00-14:00
text: Closed message in English
text: رسالة الإغلاق بالعربية | lang: ar
```

- `hours:` content → IANA timezone (e.g. `Asia/Qatar`, `Africa/Cairo`, `Europe/London`, `UTC`)
- Day range properties: `Mon-Fri`, `Sat`, `Sun`, `Mon-Thu`, `Mon-Sat`, etc. → `HH:MM-HH:MM`
- `text:` → closed message (English by default)
- `text:` with `lang: ar` property → Arabic closed message

Day abbreviations accepted: `Mon`, `Tue`, `Wed`, `Thu`, `Fri`, `Sat`, `Sun`
Ranges: `Mon-Fri` means Monday through Friday inclusive.

Section aliases: `Business Hours`, `Working Hours`, `Opening Hours`

**Example:**

```
section: Hours
hours: Asia/Qatar | Mon-Fri: 09:00-18:00 | Sat: 10:00-14:00
text: We are currently closed. Our team will respond when we open.
text: نحن مغلقون حالياً. سيرد فريقنا عند فتح الدوام. | lang: ar
```

---

### section: Skills

Named in-process tool bundles registered via `SkillRegistry`. No credentials needed.

**Blocks:**

```
skill: skill-name
```

- `skill:` content → name of a registered skill (e.g. `booking`, `payments`, `knowledge`)
- Skills must be registered in code via `SkillRegistry.register("name", factory)` before use.

**Example:**

```
section: Skills
skill: booking
skill: payments
skill: knowledge
```

---

### section: Equipment

External API connectors with credentials.

**Blocks:**

```
connector: connector-type | operations: op1,op2,op3 | access: read | endpoint: ${URL} | key: ${API_KEY}
connector: connector-type | operations: op1,op2 | access: readwrite | endpoint: ${URL} | token: ${TOKEN}
```

Followed optionally by a dedicated tools declaration:

```
connector: fresha | operations: bookings.list,bookings.create | access: readwrite | endpoint: ${FRESHA_URL} | token: ${FRESHA_TOKEN} | dedicatedTools: generate_quote,escalate_to_human
```

**`connector:` properties:**

| Property | Values | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `operations:` | comma-separated list | API operations this connector exposes (e.g. `orders.list,orders.get`) |
| `access:` | `read` \| `readwrite` | Permission level |
| `endpoint:` or `url:` | URL string | API base URL (use `${ENV_VAR}` for secrets) |
| `key:` or `apikey:` | string | API key credential value |
| `token:` | string | Bearer token credential value |
| `password:` | string | Basic auth password credential value |
| `header:` or `headername:` | string | Custom header name for the credential |
| `credentials:` | `api_key` \| `bearer` \| `basic` | Explicit credential type (auto-detected from which key is used) |
| `dedicatedTools:` or `tools:` | comma-separated list | Extra tool names available alongside this connector |

**Credential type auto-detection:**
- If `token:` is used → `bearer`
- If `password:` is used → `basic`
- If `key:` or `apikey:` is used → `api_key` (default)

**Alternative — separate credential block (attaches to the last connector):**

```
connector: shopify | operations: orders.list,customers.get | access: read | endpoint: ${SHOPIFY_ENDPOINT}
credential: api_key | value: ${SHOPIFY_API_KEY} | header: X-Shopify-Access-Token
```

**Example:**

```
section: Equipment
connector: shopify | operations: orders.list,orders.get,customers.get | access: read | endpoint: ${SHOPIFY_ENDPOINT} | key: ${SHOPIFY_API_KEY}
connector: fresha | operations: bookings.list,bookings.create,bookings.update | access: readwrite | endpoint: ${FRESHA_ENDPOINT} | token: ${FRESHA_TOKEN} | dedicatedTools: generate_quote,escalate_to_human
```

---

### section: Memory

Topics or categories the agent should remember across sessions.

**Blocks:**

```
memory: topic or category to retain
```

**Example:**

```
section: Memory
memory: customer preferences
memory: past order issues
memory: previous escalations
```

---

## Complete Example — E-commerce Support Agent

```
title: Support Agent
summary: E-commerce customer support for Gulf region

section: Persona
prompt: Nour | style: warm, direct, solution-focused | language: Arabic, English

section: Capabilities
tool: answer product questions
tool: check order status
tool: create support tickets
tool: process return requests
tool: escalate billing disputes | to: human
tool: escalate complex cases | to: human

section: Brain
model: gpt-4o-mini | provider: openai

section: Rules
policy: never fabricate order details or delivery dates
policy: always respond in the same language as the user
policy: do not share one customer's data with another customer
policy: escalate immediately if the user mentions legal action

section: Limits
info: max iterations | max: 8
info: confidence threshold | confidence: 0.65
info: cost cap | cost: 0.05

section: Hours
hours: Asia/Qatar | Mon-Fri: 09:00-18:00 | Sat: 10:00-14:00
text: We are currently closed. We will respond first thing when we open.
text: نحن مغلقون حالياً. سيتواصل معك فريقنا فور فتح الدوام. | lang: ar

section: Skills
skill: knowledge

section: Equipment
connector: shopify | operations: orders.list,orders.get,customers.get,refunds.create | access: readwrite | endpoint: ${SHOPIFY_ENDPOINT} | key: ${SHOPIFY_API_KEY}

section: Memory
memory: customer preferences
memory: previous complaints
```

---

## Complete Example — Government Inquiry Agent (Sovereign / Ollama)

```
title: Citizen Services Agent
summary: Answers public service inquiries in Arabic and English

section: Persona
prompt: Maha | style: formal, clear, patient | language: Arabic, English

section: Capabilities
tool: answer public service inquiries
tool: explain application procedures and requirements
tool: look up office locations and contact details
tool: escalate complex cases | to: human officer

section: Brain
model: phi4-mini | provider: ollama | endpoint: http://ollama:11434 | language: arabic

section: Rules
policy: never fabricate policy details or legal requirements
policy: respond in the same language as the user
policy: escalate when the query requires a human decision
policy: do not provide legal advice

section: Limits
info: max iterations | max: 6
info: confidence threshold | confidence: 0.70

section: Hours
hours: Asia/Riyadh | Sun-Thu: 08:00-16:00
text: Government offices are closed. Please call back during working hours Sun–Thu 8am–4pm.
text: الدوائر الحكومية مغلقة. يرجى التواصل خلال أوقات الدوام الأحد-الخميس 8 صباحاً-4 مساءً. | lang: ar
```

---

## Complete Example — Booking Agent (Skills + Equipment)

```
title: Salon Booking Agent
summary: Handles appointment bookings and customer queries for a beauty salon chain

section: Persona
prompt: Layla | style: friendly, efficient | language: Arabic, English

section: Capabilities
tool: check available appointment slots
tool: book an appointment
tool: cancel or reschedule an appointment
tool: answer questions about services and pricing
tool: escalate complaints | to: human

section: Brain
model: gpt-4o-mini | provider: openai

section: Rules
policy: confirm all details before booking — service, date, time, stylist
policy: never double-book a slot
policy: respond in the user's language

section: Limits
info: max iterations | max: 10
info: confidence threshold | confidence: 0.6

section: Hours
hours: Asia/Dubai | Mon-Sat: 09:00-21:00 | Sun: 10:00-18:00
text: We are closed right now. Book online or message us — we'll confirm first thing when we open!

section: Skills
skill: booking
skill: payments

section: Equipment
connector: fresha | operations: bookings.list,bookings.create,bookings.update,bookings.cancel,services.list | access: readwrite | endpoint: ${FRESHA_ENDPOINT} | token: ${FRESHA_TOKEN}
```

---

## Section Aliases (case-insensitive)

| Write this | Treated as |
|---|---|
| `Identity` | `Persona` |
| `Can Do` | `Capabilities` |
| `Constraints` | `Rules` |
| `Behaviour` / `Behavior` | `Rules` |
| `LLM` | `Brain` |
| `Model` | `Brain` |
| `Business Hours` | `Hours` |
| `Working Hours` | `Hours` |
| `Opening Hours` | `Hours` |

---

## What NOT to do

- Do not nest blocks — all lines are flat within a section.
- Do not use YAML syntax (`-` list items) — use one `tool:` / `skill:` / `policy:` line per item.
- Do not put credentials directly in the file — always use `${ENV_VAR}` references.
- Do not omit `section:` headers — blocks outside sections are ignored.
- Do not use markdown headers (`##`) — this is not a Markdown file.
- The `model:` section alias (treated as `Brain`) means you cannot name a custom section "model".

---

## Minimal Valid File

```
title: My Agent

section: Persona
prompt: Alex | style: helpful

section: Brain
model: gpt-4o-mini | provider: openai
```

All sections except `title` and at least one section are optional.
The agent will load and run with only a brain configured.

---

## Runtime Behaviour Mapping

| .it construct | AgentDefinition field | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `title:` | `name` | Agent identity in logs and health endpoint |
| `summary:` | `domain` | Injected into system prompt as domain context |
| `prompt: Name` | `persona.name` | Agent's name in persona prompt |
| `style:` property | `persona.style` | Communication style in persona prompt |
| `language:` property | `persona.language[]` | Languages listed in persona prompt |
| `tool:` line | `capabilities[]` | Tool/capability listed in every brain call |
| `model:` line | `brain.model` + `brain.provider` | LLM used for reasoning |
| `language: arabic` on brain | `brain.language` | Activates Arabic routing brain |
| `policy:` line | `rules[]` | Rules injected verbatim into system prompt |
| `info: ... | max:` | `limits.iterations` or `limits.toolCalls` | Hard loop/tool budget |
| `info: ... | confidence:` | `limits.confidenceThreshold` | Confidence gate threshold |
| `info: ... | cost:` | `limits.costCapPerTask` | Cost hard limit in USD |
| `hours:` block | `hours.timezone` + `hours.schedule[]` | Business hours gate |
| `text:` in Hours | `hours.closedMessage` / `closedMessageAr` | Gate response message |
| `skill:` line | `skills[]` | Skill names for SkillRegistry lookup |
| `connector:` line | `equipment.connectors[]` | External API connector |
| `memory:` line | `memory.retain[]` | Topics to retain in semantic memory |
